description: simplify this page quite a bit. Instead of saying discover up top, it should say the actual question that's being asked. And then in the top right, there should be an icon, a questions icon that we can open it and we can select a question. Questions, we should not say any of the details about the answers. It should just say, what is the question and the number of answers? And then selecting that changes which question we're then scrolling through. So if we hit the end of a question when scrolling, it should automatically go to the next recommended question. And the first, when we switch to a new question, the first thing that should happen is the question should be read. There should be a screen, a solid screen that just reads the question. And that is the feat. Also, you can get rid of all the other tabs. We don't need any of those.

results.summary: Discover is now one question at a time: the question is the title, a picker in the top right switches questions, each one opens with a full-screen card that reads it aloud, and the tabs are gone.
results.identified_issue: Rebuilt Discover around the question instead of around the clip. Key decisions a future agent should know: (1) The feed is a list of PAGES, not clips — DiscoverPage is .question(Question) or .clip(ArgumentClip). The LAST page of a question's feed is the NEXT question's card; scrolling onto it is what performs the handoff (select(questionID:completingCurrent:)), and because a question card's id IS the question's id, the same card is then page 0 of the new feed, so the scroll re-anchors instead of jumping. This is the load-bearing trick — if the transition looks wrong on device, that's where to look. (2) ClipPreviewPlayer was generalized from ArgumentClip to a PlayerItem (id + dedupeKey + text + optional audioURL), so the question card reads the prompt through the exact same TTS path as a text answer and inherits the same gates for free. finishedClipID became finishedItemID. (3) ArgumentClip.id is now the answer's id rather than a fresh UUID per build — that is what makes heard-state and downloaded audio segments survive a deck rebuild, and it let me delete the persisted deck entirely (the cache now stores only the listener's place: selected question, current page, heard pages, completed questions). Old snapshots simply fail to decode and the feed rebuilds — self-healing. (4) I gated the question card the same way clips are gated (must be heard before you can scroll past). That was an inference from the existing product rule, not something the voice note asked for; if it feels heavy-handed, that gate is the thing to relax. (5) I also auto-advance when the last clip finishes playing, not only when the user scrolls — the note said 'automatically', but if that yank feels wrong, delete advanceIfQuestionFinished and the scroll-triggered handoff still works on its own. (6) I did NOT delete QuestionsView/SubmitAnswerView — removing the tabs removed their only entry point, so adding a question or dictating an answer is currently unreachable in-app. The code is intact and one button away from coming back. Verified by typechecking all app sources against the iOS 17 simulator SDK (clean); NOT verified on device — the scroll re-anchor at the question handoff is the thing to watch.
results.detailed_description:
I rebuilt the page around the question rather than around the clip.

What you'll see: the title bar now shows the actual question being argued instead of the word 'Discover', and a questions icon sits in the top right. Tapping it opens a list that says only what each question is and how many answers it has — no side labels, no answer text, nothing about the answers themselves, as you asked. Picking one switches the feed to that question.

Every question now opens with a solid full-screen card that does nothing but read the question out loud. You hear what's being argued before you hear anyone argue it. When you reach the end of a question's viewpoints, the feed rolls straight into the next recommended question and starts with that question's card, so it keeps going on its own. 'Recommended' means: questions with both sides represented first, then the ones with the most answers, skipping ones you've already listened through until you've heard them all.

The tabs are gone — the app is just the feed now. One consequence worth flagging: the Answer and Questions tabs were the only way to add a question or dictate an answer, so that's currently unreachable in the app. I left all that code intact rather than deleting it, so it's one button away from coming back wherever you want it. Say where and I'll wire it up. (The build hash that used to live on the Rules tab now sits at the bottom of the question picker so you can still tell which build you're on.)

Under the hood I also simplified: the player used to only understand clips, so I taught it to play any 'page' — that's how the question card reads the prompt through the same speech path as a text answer, and it inherits the existing rules for free (no scrubbing, no skip-forward, and you can't scroll past something you haven't finished). I applied that same gate to the question card, so the question has to finish reading before you can scroll into the arguments; that was my call, not something you asked for, so tell me if it feels heavy-handed.

Proven by typechecking every app source file against the iOS 17 SDK — clean, no errors. I have not run it on device (that's vigil's job from this commit). The thing I'd watch when you test it: the moment one question hands off to the next, since that's where the feed re-roots itself around the new question.
